text: When designing a REST API, following best practices ensures it is easy to use and maintain. First, structure your endpoints logically around resources, for example using `/users` to access user data. Use meaningful names and avoid verbs, keeping them simple and consistent. Proper status codes are essential for clear communication; use 200 for success, 201 when a resource is created, 400 for bad requests, and 404 if something is not found. Always document your API thoroughly, explaining endpoints, parameters, and expected responses. This helps developers integrate quickly. Finally, implement versioning, such as `/v1/users`, so you can make updates without breaking existing clients. Good documentation and careful versioning keep your API reliable over time.
